zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Stephen Holm於1207年出生在英國素可維斯坦普內特時，他的父親John Holmes21歲，母親Katherine Lascelles17歲。他於1226年12月24日與Milicent de Sutton結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1254年1月2日享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Stephen Holm于1207年出生在英国素可維斯坦普內特时，他的父亲John Holmes21岁，母亲Katherine Lascelles17岁。他于1226年12月24日与Milicent de Sutton结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1254年1月2日享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Holm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Holme
meanings: English (northwestern England): variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.
Danish and Swedish: variant of   Holm   1.

variants.name: Homer
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a holly bush, from Middle English holm (Old English holen) + the agent suffix -er, a type of formation characteristic of Hampshire and Sussex. See Holm 2.

English: habitational name from Holmer (Herefordshire), Holmer Green (Buckinghamshire), or Homer (Devon), or else from other unrecorded placenames formed with Middle English hol(g)h + mere ‘pool by or in the hollow’ (Old English holh + mere).

English: perhaps an occupational name for a maker of helmets, from Old French heaumier, heumier (from ancient Germanic he(a)ume ‘helmet’), but it is uncertain that this name became hereditary. Compare Helm 3.

variants.name: Hollis
meanings: English (Midlands): topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) holly or holly trees’, from Middle English holi(e), holin (Old English holegn, holen). Compare Hollen and Holley 3.

variants.name: Holmen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Britt, Agnar, Bjorn, Holger, Juel.
Norwegian and Danish: variant of   Holm  , from the definite singular form.

Swedish (mainly Holmén): ornamental, topographic, or a habitational name formed with the (placename) element holm(e) ‘small island’ + the adjectival suffix -én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Hulme
meanings: English: variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.

nameMeanings.name: Stephen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , preserving the vowels of the Greek name.

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , reflecting the normal pronunciation of the name in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Stephen Holm was born in 1207, in Westhampnett, Sussex, England, his father, John Holmes, was 21 and his mother, Katherine Lascelles, was 17. He married Milicent de Sutton on 24 December 1226. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 2 January 1254, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Stephen Holm est né en 1207, à Westhampnett, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, John Holmes, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Katherine Lascelles, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Milicent de Sutton le 24 décembre 1226. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 2 janvier 1254, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Stephen Holm 1207 in Westhampnett, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Holmes 21 und seine Mutter Katherine Lascelles 17. Er hat am 24. Dezember 1226 Milicent de Sutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 2. Januar 1254 im Alter von 47 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Stephen Holm è nato il nel 1207 nel Westhampnett, Sussex, England, il suo padre, John Holmes, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Lascelles, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Milicent de Sutton il 24 dicembre 1226. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 2 gennaio 1254, all'età di 47 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Stephen Holm(이)가 1207년 영국 수세스 웨스섬p넷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Holmes(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Lascelles(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1226년 12월 24일 Milicent de Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1254년 1월 2일 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Stephen Holm родился в 1207 г. в местности вестампнетт, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Holmes было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Katherine Lascelles, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Milicent de Sutton 24 декабря 1226 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 2 января 1254 г. в возрасте 47 лет.
